title: dedup + group systemtest auto-tickets, archive seeded fixtures
description:
## Summary
- 47 of 48 open tickets on mentolder were duplicate auto-tickets from the failure-bridge (36× "Login mit Magic-Link", 11× "[TEST] cleanup"). The bridge's idempotency relied on `questionnaire_test_status` which silently failed; the bridge also wrongly flagged tickets from `is_test_data=true` assignments as real.
- New dedup is at the ticket level via partial UNIQUE on `source_test_question_id` for open rows; new tickets get `component='systemtest'`, `severity='minor'`, `is_test_data` propagated from the assignment, and a `parent_id` to a per-template **title-slug** epic (the seeder clones template UUIDs per run, so id-based grouping is useless).
- Legacy `/admin/bugs` redirects to `/admin/tickets`, which already supports the grouping fields.

## Live cleanup (already applied to mentolder)
```
Pre:  48 open bugs (47 systemtest noise + 1 real)
Post: 1  open bug  (T000073 — only real one)
      2  systemtest epics  (per template title)
      47 fixtures archived as resolution='obsolete'
```

## Test plan
- [x] Type-check clean for changed files (`astro check` — 18 unrelated pre-existing errors elsewhere)
- [x] Cleanup SQL applied to mentolder; sanity report confirms expected counts
- [x] failure-bridge.test.ts updated: fixture's `is_test_data=true` assignment now correctly produces `is_test_data=true` ticket; new assertions for `parent_id`, `component`, epic shape
- [ ] After deploy: `/admin/tickets` on mentolder shows 1 real triage ticket
- [ ] Next failed system test creates ticket under existing epic, no duplicates

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
